ayahs.textArabic: تَبَارَكَ ٱلَّذِي نَزَّلَ ٱلۡفُرۡقَانَ عَلَىٰ عَبۡدِهِۦ لِيَكُونَ لِلۡعَٰلَمِينَ نَذِيرًا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Tabaraka allathee nazzala alfurqana AAala AAabdihi liyakoona lilAAalameena natheeran
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Blessed is He who sent down the Criterion upon His Servant that he may be to the worlds a warner -
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Blessed is the One Who sent down the Standard to His servant, so that he may be a warner to the whole world.

ayahs.textArabic: ٱلَّذِي لَهُۥ مُلۡكُ ٱلسَّمَٰوَٰتِ وَٱلۡأَرۡضِ وَلَمۡ يَتَّخِذۡ وَلَدٗا وَلَمۡ يَكُن لَّهُۥ شَرِيكٞ فِي ٱلۡمُلۡكِ وَخَلَقَ كُلَّ شَيۡءٖ فَقَدَّرَهُۥ تَقۡدِيرٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Allathee lahu mulku alssamawati waalardi walam yattakhith waladan walam yakun lahu shareekun fee almulki wakhalaqa kulla shayin faqaddarahu taqdeeran
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: He to whom belongs the dominion of the heavens and the earth and who has not taken a son and has not had a partner in dominion and has created each thing and determined it with [precise] determination.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹Allah is˺ the One to Whom belongs the kingdom of the heavens and the earth, Who has never had ˹any˺ offspring, nor does He have a partner in ˹governing˺ the kingdom. He has created everything, ordaining it precisely.

ayahs.textArabic: وَٱتَّخَذُواْ مِن دُونِهِۦٓ ءَالِهَةٗ لَّا يَخۡلُقُونَ شَيۡـٔٗا وَهُمۡ يُخۡلَقُونَ وَلَا يَمۡلِكُونَ لِأَنفُسِهِمۡ ضَرّٗا وَلَا نَفۡعٗا وَلَا يَمۡلِكُونَ مَوۡتٗا وَلَا حَيَوٰةٗ وَلَا نُشُورٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waittakhathoo min doonihi alihatan la yakhluqoona shayan wahum yukhlaqoona wala yamlikoona lianfusihim darran wala nafAAan wala yamlikoona mawtan wala hayatan wala nushooran
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: But they have taken besides Him gods which create nothing, while they are created, and possess not for themselves any harm or benefit and possess not [power to cause] death or life or resurrection.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Yet they have taken besides Him gods who cannot create anything but are themselves created. Nor can they protect or benefit themselves. Nor can they control life, death, or resurrection.

ayahs.textArabic: وَقَالَ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوٓاْ إِنۡ هَٰذَآ إِلَّآ إِفۡكٌ ٱفۡتَرَىٰهُ وَأَعَانَهُۥ عَلَيۡهِ قَوۡمٌ ءَاخَرُونَۖ فَقَدۡ جَآءُو ظُلۡمٗا وَزُورٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waqala allatheena kafaroo in hatha illa ifkun iftarahu waaAAanahu AAalayhi qawmun akharoona faqad jaoo thulman wazooran
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And those who disbelieve say, "This [Qur'an] is not except a falsehood he invented, and another people assisted him in it." But they have committed an injustice and a lie.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: The disbelievers say, “This ˹Quran˺ is nothing but a fabrication which he made up with the help of others.” Their claim is totally unjustified and untrue!

ayahs.textArabic: وَقَالُوٓاْ أَسَٰطِيرُ ٱلۡأَوَّلِينَ ٱكۡتَتَبَهَا فَهِيَ تُمۡلَىٰ عَلَيۡهِ بُكۡرَةٗ وَأَصِيلٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waqaloo asateeru alawwaleena iktatabaha fahiya tumla AAalayhi bukratan waaseelan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And they say, "Legends of the former peoples which he has written down, and they are dictated to him morning and afternoon."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And they say, “˹These revelations are only˺ ancient fables which he has had written down, and they are rehearsed to him morning and evening.”

ayahs.textArabic: قُلۡ أَنزَلَهُ ٱلَّذِي يَعۡلَمُ ٱلسِّرَّ فِي ٱلسَّمَٰوَٰتِ وَٱلۡأَرۡضِۚ إِنَّهُۥ كَانَ غَفُورٗا رَّحِيمٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qul anzalahu allathee yaAAlamu alssirra fee alssamawati waalardi innahu kana ghafooran raheeman
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Say, [O Muhammad], "It has been revealed by He who knows [every] secret within the heavens and the earth. Indeed, He is ever Forgiving and Merciful."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Say, ˹O Prophet,˺ “This ˹Quran˺ has been revealed by the One Who knows the secrets of the heavens and the earth. Surely He is All-Forgiving, Most Merciful.”

ayahs.textArabic: وَقَالُواْ مَالِ هَٰذَا ٱلرَّسُولِ يَأۡكُلُ ٱلطَّعَامَ وَيَمۡشِي فِي ٱلۡأَسۡوَاقِ لَوۡلَآ أُنزِلَ إِلَيۡهِ مَلَكٞ فَيَكُونَ مَعَهُۥ نَذِيرًا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waqaloo mali hatha alrrasooli yakulu alttaAAama wayamshee fee alaswaqi lawla onzila ilayhi malakun fayakoona maAAahu natheeran
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And they say, "What is this messenger that eats food and walks in the markets? Why was there not sent down to him an angel so he would be with him a warner?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And they say ˹mockingly˺, “What kind of messenger is this who eats food and goes about in market-places ˹for a living˺? If only an angel had been sent down with him to be his co-warner,

ayahs.textArabic: أَوۡ يُلۡقَىٰٓ إِلَيۡهِ كَنزٌ أَوۡ تَكُونُ لَهُۥ جَنَّةٞ يَأۡكُلُ مِنۡهَاۚ وَقَالَ ٱلظَّٰلِمُونَ إِن تَتَّبِعُونَ إِلَّا رَجُلٗا مَّسۡحُورًا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Aw yulqa ilayhi kanzun aw takoonu lahu jannatun yakulu minha waqala alththalimoona in tattabiAAoona illa rajulan mashooran
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Or [why is not] a treasure presented to him [from heaven], or does he [not] have a garden from which he eats?" And the wrongdoers say, "You follow not but a man affected by magic."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: or a treasure had been cast down to him, or he had had a garden from which he may eat!” And the wrongdoers say ˹to the believers˺, “You are only following a bewitched man.”

ayahs.textArabic: ٱنظُرۡ كَيۡفَ ضَرَبُواْ لَكَ ٱلۡأَمۡثَٰلَ فَضَلُّواْ فَلَا يَسۡتَطِيعُونَ سَبِيلٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Onthur kayfa daraboo laka alamthala fadalloo fala yastateeAAoona sabeelan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Look how they strike for you comparisons; but they have strayed, so they cannot [find] a way.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: See ˹O Prophet˺ how they call you names! So they have gone so ˹far˺ astray that they cannot find the ˹Right˺ Way.

ayahs.textArabic: تَبَارَكَ ٱلَّذِيٓ إِن شَآءَ جَعَلَ لَكَ خَيۡرٗا مِّن ذَٰلِكَ جَنَّٰتٖ تَجۡرِي مِن تَحۡتِهَا ٱلۡأَنۡهَٰرُ وَيَجۡعَل لَّكَ قُصُورَۢا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Tabaraka allathee in shaa jaAAala laka khayran min thalika jannatin tajree min tahtiha alanharu wayajAAal laka qusooran
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Blessed is He who, if He willed, could have made for you [something] better than that - gardens beneath which rivers flow - and could make for you palaces.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Blessed is the One Who—if He wills—can give you far better than ˹all˺ that: gardens under which rivers flow, and palaces as well.

ayahs.textArabic: بَلۡ كَذَّبُواْ بِٱلسَّاعَةِۖ وَأَعۡتَدۡنَا لِمَن كَذَّبَ بِٱلسَّاعَةِ سَعِيرًا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Bal kaththaboo bialssaAAati waaAAtadna liman kaththaba bialssaAAati saAAeeran
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: But they have denied the Hour, and We have prepared for those who deny the Hour a Blaze.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: In fact, they deny the Hour. And for the deniers of the Hour, We have prepared a blazing Fire.

ayahs.textArabic: إِذَا رَأَتۡهُم مِّن مَّكَانِۭ بَعِيدٖ سَمِعُواْ لَهَا تَغَيُّظٗا وَزَفِيرٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Itha raathum min makanin baAAeedin samiAAoo laha taghayyuthan wazafeeran
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: When the Hellfire sees them from a distant place, they will hear its fury and roaring.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Once it sees them from a distance, they will hear it fuming and growling.

ayahs.textArabic: وَإِذَآ أُلۡقُواْ مِنۡهَا مَكَانٗا ضَيِّقٗا مُّقَرَّنِينَ دَعَوۡاْ هُنَالِكَ ثُبُورٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waitha olqoo minha makanan dayyiqan muqarraneena daAAaw hunalika thubooran
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And when they are thrown into a narrow place therein bound in chains, they will cry out thereupon for destruction.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And when they are tossed into a narrow place inside ˹Hell˺, chained together, then and there they will cry out for ˹instant˺ destruction.

ayahs.textArabic: لَّا تَدۡعُواْ ٱلۡيَوۡمَ ثُبُورٗا وَٰحِدٗا وَٱدۡعُواْ ثُبُورٗا كَثِيرٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: La tadAAoo alyawma thubooran wahidan waodAAoo thubooran katheeran
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [They will be told], "Do not cry this Day for one destruction but cry for much destruction."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹They will be told,˺ “Do not cry only once for destruction, but cry many times over!”

ayahs.textArabic: قُلۡ أَذَٰلِكَ خَيۡرٌ أَمۡ جَنَّةُ ٱلۡخُلۡدِ ٱلَّتِي وُعِدَ ٱلۡمُتَّقُونَۚ كَانَتۡ لَهُمۡ جَزَآءٗ وَمَصِيرٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qul athalika khayrun am jannatu alkhuldi allatee wuAAida almuttaqoona kanat lahum jazaan wamaseeran
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Say, "Is that better or the Garden of Eternity which is promised to the righteous? It will be for them a reward and destination.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Say, ˹O Prophet,˺ “Is this better or the Garden of Eternity which the righteous have been promised, as a reward and ˹an ultimate˺ destination?

ayahs.textArabic: لَّهُمۡ فِيهَا مَا يَشَآءُونَ خَٰلِدِينَۚ كَانَ عَلَىٰ رَبِّكَ وَعۡدٗا مَّسۡـُٔولٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Lahum feeha ma yashaoona khalideena kana AAala rabbika waAAdan masoolan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: For them therein is whatever they wish, [while] abiding eternally. It is ever upon your Lord a promise [worthy to be] requested.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: There they will have whatever they wish for, forever. That is a promise ˹to be sought after˺, binding on your Lord.”

ayahs.textArabic: وَيَوۡمَ يَحۡشُرُهُمۡ وَمَا يَعۡبُدُونَ مِن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ فَيَقُولُ ءَأَنتُمۡ أَضۡلَلۡتُمۡ عِبَادِي هَٰٓؤُلَآءِ أَمۡ هُمۡ ضَلُّواْ ٱلسَّبِيلَ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wayawma yahshuruhum wama yaAAbudoona min dooni Allahi fayaqoolu aantum adlaltum AAibadee haolai am hum dalloo alssabeela
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And [mention] the Day He will gather them and that which they worship besides Allah and will say, "Did you mislead these, My servants, or did they [themselves] stray from the way?"
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹Watch for˺ the Day He will gather them along with what they used to worship besides Allah, and ask ˹the objects of worship˺, “Was it you who misled these servants of Mine, or did they stray from the Way ˹on their own˺?”

ayahs.textArabic: قَالُواْ سُبۡحَٰنَكَ مَا كَانَ يَنۢبَغِي لَنَآ أَن نَّتَّخِذَ مِن دُونِكَ مِنۡ أَوۡلِيَآءَ وَلَٰكِن مَّتَّعۡتَهُمۡ وَءَابَآءَهُمۡ حَتَّىٰ نَسُواْ ٱلذِّكۡرَ وَكَانُواْ قَوۡمَۢا بُورٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qaloo subhanaka ma kana yanbaghee lana an nattakhitha min doonika min awliyaa walakin mattaAAtahum waabaahum hatta nasoo alththikra wakanoo qawman booran
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: They will say, "Exalted are You! It was not for us to take besides You any allies. But You provided comforts for them and their fathers until they forgot the message and became a people ruined."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: They will say, “Glory be to You! It was not right for ˹others like˺ us to take any lords besides You, but You allowed enjoyment for them and their forefathers ˹for so long˺ that they forgot ˹Your˺ remembrance and became a doomed people.”

ayahs.textArabic: فَقَدۡ كَذَّبُوكُم بِمَا تَقُولُونَ فَمَا تَسۡتَطِيعُونَ صَرۡفٗا وَلَا نَصۡرٗاۚ وَمَن يَظۡلِم مِّنكُمۡ نُذِقۡهُ عَذَابٗا كَبِيرٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Faqad kaththabookum bima taqooloona fama tastateeAAoona sarfan wala nasran waman yathlim minkum nuthiqhu AAathaban kabeeran
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So they will deny you, [disbelievers], in what you say, and you cannot avert [punishment] or [find] help. And whoever commits injustice among you - We will make him taste a great punishment.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹The doomed will be told˺, “Your gods have clearly denied your claims. So now you can neither ward off ˹the punishment˺ nor get any help.” And whoever of you does wrong, We will make them taste a horrible punishment.

ayahs.textArabic: وَمَآ أَرۡسَلۡنَا قَبۡلَكَ مِنَ ٱلۡمُرۡسَلِينَ إِلَّآ إِنَّهُمۡ لَيَأۡكُلُونَ ٱلطَّعَامَ وَيَمۡشُونَ فِي ٱلۡأَسۡوَاقِۗ وَجَعَلۡنَا بَعۡضَكُمۡ لِبَعۡضٖ فِتۡنَةً أَتَصۡبِرُونَۗ وَكَانَ رَبُّكَ بَصِيرٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wama arsalna qablaka mina almursaleena illa innahum layakuloona alttaAAama wayamshoona fee alaswaqi wajaAAalna baAAdakum libaAAdin fitnatan atasbiroona wakana rabbuka baseeran
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And We did not send before you, [O Muhammad], any of the messengers except that they ate food and walked in the markets. And We have made some of you [people] as trial for others - will you have patience? And ever is your Lord, Seeing.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: We never sent any messenger before you ˹O Prophet˺, who did not eat food and go about in market-places. We have made some of you a trial for others. Will you ˹not then˺ be patient? And your Lord is All-Seeing.

ayahs.textArabic: ۞ وَقَالَ ٱلَّذِينَ لَا يَرۡجُونَ لِقَآءَنَا لَوۡلَآ أُنزِلَ عَلَيۡنَا ٱلۡمَلَٰٓئِكَةُ أَوۡ نَرَىٰ رَبَّنَاۗ لَقَدِ ٱسۡتَكۡبَرُواْ فِيٓ أَنفُسِهِمۡ وَعَتَوۡ عُتُوّٗا كَبِيرٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waqala allatheena la yarjoona liqaana lawla onzila AAalayna almalaikatu aw nara rabbana laqadi istakbaroo fee anfusihim waAAataw AAutuwwan kabeeran
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And those who do not expect the meeting with Us say, "Why were not angels sent down to us, or [why] do we [not] see our Lord?" They have certainly become arrogant within themselves and [become] insolent with great insolence.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Those who do not expect to meet Us say, “If only the angels were sent down to us, or we could see our Lord!” They have certainly been carried away by their arrogance and have entirely exceeded all limits.

ayahs.textArabic: يَوۡمَ يَرَوۡنَ ٱلۡمَلَٰٓئِكَةَ لَا بُشۡرَىٰ يَوۡمَئِذٖ لِّلۡمُجۡرِمِينَ وَيَقُولُونَ حِجۡرٗا مَّحۡجُورٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Yawma yarawna almalaikata la bushra yawmaithin lilmujrimeena wayaqooloona hijran mahjooran
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: The day they see the angels - no good tidings will there be that day for the criminals, and [the angels] will say, "Prevented and inaccessible."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹But˺ on the Day they will see the angels, there will be no good news for the wicked, who will cry, “Keep away! Away ˹from us˺!”

ayahs.textArabic: وَقَدِمۡنَآ إِلَىٰ مَا عَمِلُواْ مِنۡ عَمَلٖ فَجَعَلۡنَٰهُ هَبَآءٗ مَّنثُورًا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waqadimna ila ma AAamiloo min AAamalin fajaAAalnahu habaan manthooran
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And We will regard what they have done of deeds and make them as dust dispersed.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Then We will turn to whatever ˹good˺ deeds they did, reducing them to scattered dust.

ayahs.textArabic: أَصۡحَٰبُ ٱلۡجَنَّةِ يَوۡمَئِذٍ خَيۡرٞ مُّسۡتَقَرّٗا وَأَحۡسَنُ مَقِيلٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Ashabu aljannati yawmaithin khayrun mustaqarran waahsanu maqeelan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: The companions of Paradise, that Day, are [in] a better settlement and better resting place.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹But˺ on that Day the residents of Paradise will have the best settlement and the finest place to rest.

ayahs.textArabic: وَيَوۡمَ تَشَقَّقُ ٱلسَّمَآءُ بِٱلۡغَمَٰمِ وَنُزِّلَ ٱلۡمَلَٰٓئِكَةُ تَنزِيلًا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wayawma tashaqqaqu alssamao bialghamami wanuzzila almalaikatu tanzeelan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And [mention] the Day when the heaven will split open with [emerging] clouds, and the angels will be sent down in successive descent.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹Watch for˺ the Day the heavens will burst with clouds, and the angels will be sent down in successive ranks.

ayahs.textArabic: ٱلۡمُلۡكُ يَوۡمَئِذٍ ٱلۡحَقُّ لِلرَّحۡمَٰنِۚ وَكَانَ يَوۡمًا عَلَى ٱلۡكَٰفِرِينَ عَسِيرٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Almulku yawmaithin alhaqqu lilrrahmani wakana yawman AAala alkafireena AAaseeran
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: True sovereignty, that Day, is for the Most Merciful. And it will be upon the disbelievers a difficult Day.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: True authority on that Day will belong ˹only˺ to the Most Compassionate. And it will be a hard day for the disbelievers.

ayahs.textArabic: وَيَوۡمَ يَعَضُّ ٱلظَّالِمُ عَلَىٰ يَدَيۡهِ يَقُولُ يَٰلَيۡتَنِي ٱتَّخَذۡتُ مَعَ ٱلرَّسُولِ سَبِيلٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wayawma yaAAaddu alththalimu AAala yadayhi yaqoolu ya laytanee ittakhathtu maAAa alrrasooli sabeelan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And the Day the wrongdoer will bite on his hands [in regret] he will say, "Oh, I wish I had taken with the Messenger a way.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And ˹beware of˺ the Day the wrongdoer will bite his nails ˹in regret˺ and say, “Oh! I wish I had followed the Way along with the Messenger!

ayahs.textArabic: يَٰوَيۡلَتَىٰ لَيۡتَنِي لَمۡ أَتَّخِذۡ فُلَانًا خَلِيلٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Ya waylata laytanee lam attakhith fulanan khaleelan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Oh, woe to me! I wish I had not taken that one as a friend.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Woe to me! I wish I had never taken so-and-so as a close friend.

ayahs.textArabic: لَّقَدۡ أَضَلَّنِي عَنِ ٱلذِّكۡرِ بَعۡدَ إِذۡ جَآءَنِيۗ وَكَانَ ٱلشَّيۡطَٰنُ لِلۡإِنسَٰنِ خَذُولٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Laqad adallanee AAani alththikri baAAda ith jaanee wakana alshshaytanu lilinsani khathoolan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: He led me away from the remembrance after it had come to me. And ever is Satan, to man, a deserter."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: It was he who truly made me stray from the Reminder after it had reached me.” And Satan has always betrayed humanity.

ayahs.textArabic: وَقَالَ ٱلرَّسُولُ يَٰرَبِّ إِنَّ قَوۡمِي ٱتَّخَذُواْ هَٰذَا ٱلۡقُرۡءَانَ مَهۡجُورٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waqala alrrasoolu ya rabbi inna qawmee ittakhathoo hatha alqurana mahjooran
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And the Messenger has said, "O my Lord, indeed my people have taken this Qur'an as [a thing] abandoned."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: The Messenger has cried, “O my Lord! My people have indeed received this Quran with neglect.”

ayahs.textArabic: وَكَذَٰلِكَ جَعَلۡنَا لِكُلِّ نَبِيٍّ عَدُوّٗا مِّنَ ٱلۡمُجۡرِمِينَۗ وَكَفَىٰ بِرَبِّكَ هَادِيٗا وَنَصِيرٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wakathalika jaAAalna likulli nabiyyin AAaduwwan mina almujrimeena wakafa birabbika hadiyan wanaseeran
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And thus have We made for every prophet an enemy from among the criminals. But sufficient is your Lord as a guide and a helper.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Similarly, We made enemies for every prophet from among the wicked, but sufficient is your Lord as a Guide and Helper.

ayahs.textArabic: وَقَالَ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُواْ لَوۡلَا نُزِّلَ عَلَيۡهِ ٱلۡقُرۡءَانُ جُمۡلَةٗ وَٰحِدَةٗۚ كَذَٰلِكَ لِنُثَبِّتَ بِهِۦ فُؤَادَكَۖ وَرَتَّلۡنَٰهُ تَرۡتِيلٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waqala allatheena kafaroo lawla nuzzila AAalayhi alquranu jumlatan wahidatan kathalika linuthabbita bihi fuadaka warattalnahu tarteelan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And those who disbelieve say, "Why was the Qur'an not revealed to him all at once?" Thus [it is] that We may strengthen thereby your heart. And We have spaced it distinctly.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: The disbelievers say, “If only the Quran had been sent down to him all at once!” ˹We have sent it˺ as such ˹in stages˺ so We may reassure your heart with it. And We have revealed it at a deliberate pace.

ayahs.textArabic: وَلَا يَأۡتُونَكَ بِمَثَلٍ إِلَّا جِئۡنَٰكَ بِٱلۡحَقِّ وَأَحۡسَنَ تَفۡسِيرًا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wala yatoonaka bimathalin illa jinaka bialhaqqi waahsana tafseeran
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And they do not come to you with an argument except that We bring you the truth and the best explanation.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Whenever they bring you an argument, We come to you with the right refutation and the best explanation.

ayahs.textArabic: ٱلَّذِينَ يُحۡشَرُونَ عَلَىٰ وُجُوهِهِمۡ إِلَىٰ جَهَنَّمَ أُوْلَٰٓئِكَ شَرّٞ مَّكَانٗا وَأَضَلُّ سَبِيلٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Allatheena yuhsharoona AAala wujoohihim ila jahannama olaika sharrun makanan waadallu sabeelan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: The ones who are gathered on their faces to Hell - those are the worst in position and farthest astray in [their] way.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Those who will be dragged into Hell on their faces will be in the worst place, and are ˹now˺ farthest from the ˹Right˺ Way.

ayahs.textArabic: وَلَقَدۡ ءَاتَيۡنَا مُوسَى ٱلۡكِتَٰبَ وَجَعَلۡنَا مَعَهُۥٓ أَخَاهُ هَٰرُونَ وَزِيرٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Walaqad atayna moosa alkitaba wajaAAalna maAAahu akhahu haroona wazeeran
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And We had certainly given Moses the Scripture and appointed with him his brother Aaron as an assistant.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: We certainly gave Moses the Book and appointed his brother Aaron as his helper.

ayahs.textArabic: فَقُلۡنَا ٱذۡهَبَآ إِلَى ٱلۡقَوۡمِ ٱلَّذِينَ كَذَّبُواْ بِـَٔايَٰتِنَا فَدَمَّرۡنَٰهُمۡ تَدۡمِيرٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Faqulna ithhaba ila alqawmi allatheena kaththaboo biayatina fadammarnahum tadmeeran
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And We said, "Go both of you to the people who have denied Our signs." Then We destroyed them with [complete] destruction.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: We had ordered ˹them˺, “Go to the people who would deny Our signs.” Then We annihilated the deniers entirely.

ayahs.textArabic: وَقَوۡمَ نُوحٖ لَّمَّا كَذَّبُواْ ٱلرُّسُلَ أَغۡرَقۡنَٰهُمۡ وَجَعَلۡنَٰهُمۡ لِلنَّاسِ ءَايَةٗۖ وَأَعۡتَدۡنَا لِلظَّٰلِمِينَ عَذَابًا أَلِيمٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waqawma noohin lamma kaththaboo alrrusula aghraqnahum wajaAAalnahum lilnnasi ayatan waaAAtadna lilththalimeena AAathaban aleeman
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And the people of Noah - when they denied the messengers, We drowned them, and We made them for mankind a sign. And We have prepared for the wrongdoers a painful punishment.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And when the people of Noah rejected the messengers, We drowned them, making them an example to humanity. And We have prepared a painful punishment for the wrongdoers.

ayahs.textArabic: وَعَادٗا وَثَمُودَاْ وَأَصۡحَٰبَ ٱلرَّسِّ وَقُرُونَۢا بَيۡنَ ذَٰلِكَ كَثِيرٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: WaAAadan wathamooda waashaba alrrassi waquroonan bayna thalika katheeran
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And [We destroyed] 'Aad and Thamud and the companions of the well and many generations between them.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Also ˹We destroyed˺ ’Ȃd, Thamûd, and the people of the Water-pit, as well as many peoples in between.

ayahs.textArabic: وَكُلّٗا ضَرَبۡنَا لَهُ ٱلۡأَمۡثَٰلَۖ وَكُلّٗا تَبَّرۡنَا تَتۡبِيرٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wakullan darabna lahu alamthala wakullan tabbarna tatbeeran
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And for each We presented examples [as warnings], and each We destroyed with [total] destruction.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: For each We set forth ˹various˺ lessons, and We ultimately destroyed each.

ayahs.textArabic: وَلَقَدۡ أَتَوۡاْ عَلَى ٱلۡقَرۡيَةِ ٱلَّتِيٓ أُمۡطِرَتۡ مَطَرَ ٱلسَّوۡءِۚ أَفَلَمۡ يَكُونُواْ يَرَوۡنَهَاۚ بَلۡ كَانُواْ لَا يَرۡجُونَ نُشُورٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Walaqad ataw AAala alqaryati allatee omtirat matara alssawi afalam yakoonoo yarawnaha bal kanoo la yarjoona nushooran
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And they have already come upon the town which was showered with a rain of evil. So have they not seen it? But they are not expecting resurrection.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: They have certainly passed by the city ˹of Sodom˺, which had been showered with a dreadful rain ˹of stones˺. Have they not seen its ruins? But they do not expect to be resurrected.

ayahs.textArabic: وَإِذَا رَأَوۡكَ إِن يَتَّخِذُونَكَ إِلَّا هُزُوًا أَهَٰذَا ٱلَّذِي بَعَثَ ٱللَّهُ رَسُولًا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waitha raawka in yattakhithoonaka illa huzuwan ahatha allathee baAAatha Allahu rasoolan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And when they see you, [O Muhammad], they take you not except in ridicule, [saying], "Is this the one whom Allah has sent as a messenger?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: When they see you ˹O Prophet˺, they only make fun of you, ˹saying,˺ “Is this the one that Allah has sent as a messenger?

ayahs.textArabic: إِن كَادَ لَيُضِلُّنَا عَنۡ ءَالِهَتِنَا لَوۡلَآ أَن صَبَرۡنَا عَلَيۡهَاۚ وَسَوۡفَ يَعۡلَمُونَ حِينَ يَرَوۡنَ ٱلۡعَذَابَ مَنۡ أَضَلُّ سَبِيلًا
ayahs.textTransliteration: In kada layudilluna AAan alihatina lawla an sabarna AAalayha wasawfa yaAAlamoona heena yarawna alAAathaba man adallu sabeelan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: He almost would have misled us from our gods had we not been steadfast in [worship of] them." But they are going to know, when they see the punishment, who is farthest astray in [his] way.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: He would have almost tricked us away from our gods, had we not been so devoted to them.” ˹But˺ soon they will know, when they face the punishment, who is far astray from the ˹Right˺ Way.

ayahs.textArabic: أَرَءَيۡتَ مَنِ ٱتَّخَذَ إِلَٰهَهُۥ هَوَىٰهُ أَفَأَنتَ تَكُونُ عَلَيۡهِ وَكِيلًا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Araayta mani ittakhatha ilahahu hawahu afaanta takoonu AAalayhi wakeelan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Have you seen the one who takes as his god his own desire? Then would you be responsible for him?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Have you seen ˹O Prophet˺ the one who has taken their own desires as their god? Will you then be a keeper over them?

ayahs.textArabic: أَمۡ تَحۡسَبُ أَنَّ أَكۡثَرَهُمۡ يَسۡمَعُونَ أَوۡ يَعۡقِلُونَۚ إِنۡ هُمۡ إِلَّا كَٱلۡأَنۡعَٰمِ بَلۡ هُمۡ أَضَلُّ سَبِيلًا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Am tahsabu anna aktharahum yasmaAAoona aw yaAAqiloona in hum illa kaalanAAami bal hum adallu sabeelan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Or do you think that most of them hear or reason? They are not except like livestock. Rather, they are [even] more astray in [their] way.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Or do you think that most of them listen or understand? They are only like cattle—no, more than that, they are astray from the ˹Right˺ Way!

ayahs.textArabic: أَلَمۡ تَرَ إِلَىٰ رَبِّكَ كَيۡفَ مَدَّ ٱلظِّلَّ وَلَوۡ شَآءَ لَجَعَلَهُۥ سَاكِنٗا ثُمَّ جَعَلۡنَا ٱلشَّمۡسَ عَلَيۡهِ دَلِيلٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Alam tara ila rabbika kayfa madda alththilla walaw shaa lajaAAalahu sakinan thumma jaAAalna alshshamsa AAalayhi daleelan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Have you not considered your Lord - how He extends the shadow, and if He willed, He could have made it stationary? Then We made the sun for it an indication.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Have you not seen how your Lord extends the shade—He could have simply made it ˹remain˺ still if He so willed—then We make the sun its guide,

ayahs.textArabic: ثُمَّ قَبَضۡنَٰهُ إِلَيۡنَا قَبۡضٗا يَسِيرٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Thumma qabadnahu ilayna qabdan yaseeran
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Then We hold it in hand for a brief grasp.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: causing the shade to retreat gradually?

ayahs.textArabic: وَهُوَ ٱلَّذِي جَعَلَ لَكُمُ ٱلَّيۡلَ لِبَاسٗا وَٱلنَّوۡمَ سُبَاتٗا وَجَعَلَ ٱلنَّهَارَ نُشُورٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wahuwa allathee jaAAala lakumu allayla libasan waalnnawma subatan wajaAAala alnnahara nushooran
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And it is He who has made the night for you as clothing and sleep [a means for] rest and has made the day a resurrection.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: He is the One Who has made the night for you as a cover, and ˹made˺ sleep for resting, and the day for rising.

ayahs.textArabic: وَهُوَ ٱلَّذِيٓ أَرۡسَلَ ٱلرِّيَٰحَ بُشۡرَۢا بَيۡنَ يَدَيۡ رَحۡمَتِهِۦۚ وَأَنزَلۡنَا مِنَ ٱلسَّمَآءِ مَآءٗ طَهُورٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wahuwa allathee arsala alrriyaha bushran bayna yaday rahmatihi waanzalna mina alssamai maan tahooran
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And it is He who sends the winds as good tidings before His mercy, and We send down from the sky pure water
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And He is the One Who sends the winds ushering in His mercy, and We send down pure rain from the sky,

ayahs.textArabic: لِّنُحۡـِۧيَ بِهِۦ بَلۡدَةٗ مَّيۡتٗا وَنُسۡقِيَهُۥ مِمَّا خَلَقۡنَآ أَنۡعَٰمٗا وَأَنَاسِيَّ كَثِيرٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Linuhyiya bihi baldatan maytan wanusqiyahu mimma khalaqna anAAaman waanasiyya katheeran
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: That We may bring to life thereby a dead land and give it as drink to those We created of numerous livestock and men.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: giving life to a lifeless land, and providing water for countless animals and humans of Our Own creation.

ayahs.textArabic: وَلَقَدۡ صَرَّفۡنَٰهُ بَيۡنَهُمۡ لِيَذَّكَّرُواْ فَأَبَىٰٓ أَكۡثَرُ ٱلنَّاسِ إِلَّا كُفُورٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Walaqad sarrafnahu baynahum liyaththakkaroo faaba aktharu alnnasi illa kufooran
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And We have certainly distributed it among them that they might be reminded, but most of the people refuse except disbelief.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: We certainly disperse it among them so they may be mindful, but most people persist in ungratefulness.

ayahs.textArabic: وَلَوۡ شِئۡنَا لَبَعَثۡنَا فِي كُلِّ قَرۡيَةٖ نَّذِيرٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Walaw shina labaAAathna fee kulli qaryatin natheeran
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And if We had willed, We could have sent into every city a warner.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Had We willed, We could have easily sent a warner to every society.

ayahs.textArabic: فَلَا تُطِعِ ٱلۡكَٰفِرِينَ وَجَٰهِدۡهُم بِهِۦ جِهَادٗا كَبِيرٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Fala tutiAAi alkafireena wajahidhum bihi jihadan kabeeran
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So do not obey the disbelievers, and strive against them with the Qur'an a great striving.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: So do not yield to the disbelievers, but strive diligently against them with this ˹Quran˺.

ayahs.textArabic: ۞ وَهُوَ ٱلَّذِي مَرَجَ ٱلۡبَحۡرَيۡنِ هَٰذَا عَذۡبٞ فُرَاتٞ وَهَٰذَا مِلۡحٌ أُجَاجٞ وَجَعَلَ بَيۡنَهُمَا بَرۡزَخٗا وَحِجۡرٗا مَّحۡجُورٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wahuwa allathee maraja albahrayni hatha AAathbun furatun wahatha milhun ojajun wajaAAala baynahuma barzakhan wahijran mahjooran
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And it is He who has released [simultaneously] the two seas, one fresh and sweet and one salty and bitter, and He placed between them a barrier and prohibiting partition.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And He is the One Who merges the two bodies of water: one fresh and palatable and the other salty and bitter, placing between them a barrier they cannot cross.

ayahs.textArabic: وَهُوَ ٱلَّذِي خَلَقَ مِنَ ٱلۡمَآءِ بَشَرٗا فَجَعَلَهُۥ نَسَبٗا وَصِهۡرٗاۗ وَكَانَ رَبُّكَ قَدِيرٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wahuwa allathee khalaqa mina almai basharan fajaAAalahu nasaban wasihran wakana rabbuka qadeeran
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And it is He who has created from water a human being and made him [a relative by] lineage and marriage. And ever is your Lord competent [concerning creation].
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And He is the One Who creates human beings from a ˹humble˺ liquid, then establishes for them bonds of kinship and marriage. For your Lord is Most Capable.

ayahs.textArabic: وَيَعۡبُدُونَ مِن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ مَا لَا يَنفَعُهُمۡ وَلَا يَضُرُّهُمۡۗ وَكَانَ ٱلۡكَافِرُ عَلَىٰ رَبِّهِۦ ظَهِيرٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: WayaAAbudoona min dooni Allahi ma la yanfaAAuhum wala yadurruhum wakana alkafiru AAala rabbihi thaheeran
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: But they worship rather than Allah that which does not benefit them or harm them, and the disbeliever is ever, against his Lord, an assistant [to Satan].
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Yet they worship besides Allah what can neither benefit nor harm them. And the disbeliever always collaborates against their Lord.

ayahs.textArabic: وَمَآ أَرۡسَلۡنَٰكَ إِلَّا مُبَشِّرٗا وَنَذِيرٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wama arsalnaka illa mubashshiran wanatheeran
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And We have not sent you, [O Muhammad], except as a bringer of good tidings and a warner.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And We have sent you ˹O Prophet˺ only as a deliverer of good news and a warner.

ayahs.textArabic: قُلۡ مَآ أَسۡـَٔلُكُمۡ عَلَيۡهِ مِنۡ أَجۡرٍ إِلَّا مَن شَآءَ أَن يَتَّخِذَ إِلَىٰ رَبِّهِۦ سَبِيلٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qul ma asalukum AAalayhi min ajrin illa man shaa an yattakhitha ila rabbihi sabeelan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Say, "I do not ask of you for it any payment - only that whoever wills might take to his Lord a way."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Say, “I do not ask you for any reward for this ˹message˺, but whoever wishes, let them pursue the Way to their Lord.”

ayahs.textArabic: وَتَوَكَّلۡ عَلَى ٱلۡحَيِّ ٱلَّذِي لَا يَمُوتُ وَسَبِّحۡ بِحَمۡدِهِۦۚ وَكَفَىٰ بِهِۦ بِذُنُوبِ عِبَادِهِۦ خَبِيرًا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Watawakkal AAala alhayyi allathee la yamootu wasabbih bihamdihi wakafa bihi bithunoobi AAibadihi khabeeran
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And rely upon the Ever-Living who does not die, and exalt [Allah] with His praise. And sufficient is He to be, with the sins of His servants, Acquainted -
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Put your trust in the Ever-Living, Who never dies, and glorify His praises. Sufficient is He as All-Aware of the sins of His servants.

ayahs.textArabic: ٱلَّذِي خَلَقَ ٱلسَّمَٰوَٰتِ وَٱلۡأَرۡضَ وَمَا بَيۡنَهُمَا فِي سِتَّةِ أَيَّامٖ ثُمَّ ٱسۡتَوَىٰ عَلَى ٱلۡعَرۡشِۖ ٱلرَّحۡمَٰنُ فَسۡـَٔلۡ بِهِۦ خَبِيرٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Allathee khalaqa alssamawati waalarda wama baynahuma fee sittati ayyamin thumma istawa AAala alAAarshi alrrahmanu faisal bihi khabeeran
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: He who created the heavens and the earth and what is between them in six days and then established Himself above the Throne - the Most Merciful, so ask about Him one well informed.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹He is˺ the One Who created the heavens and the earth and everything in between in six Days, then established Himself on the Throne. ˹He is˺ the Most Compassionate! Ask ˹none other than˺ the All-Knowledgeable about Himself.

ayahs.textArabic: وَإِذَا قِيلَ لَهُمُ ٱسۡجُدُواْۤ لِلرَّحۡمَٰنِ قَالُواْ وَمَا ٱلرَّحۡمَٰنُ أَنَسۡجُدُ لِمَا تَأۡمُرُنَا وَزَادَهُمۡ نُفُورٗا۩
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waitha qeela lahumu osjudoo lilrrahmani qaloo wama alrrahmanu anasjudu lima tamuruna wazadahum nufooran
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And when it is said to them, "Prostrate to the Most Merciful," they say, "And what is the Most Merciful? Should we prostrate to that which you order us?" And it increases them in aversion.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: When it is said to them, “Prostrate to the Most Compassionate,” they ask ˹in disgust˺, “What is ‘the Most Compassionate’? Will we prostrate to whatever you order us to?” And it only drives them farther away.

ayahs.textArabic: تَبَارَكَ ٱلَّذِي جَعَلَ فِي ٱلسَّمَآءِ بُرُوجٗا وَجَعَلَ فِيهَا سِرَٰجٗا وَقَمَرٗا مُّنِيرٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Tabaraka allathee jaAAala fee alssamai buroojan wajaAAala feeha sirajan waqamaran muneeran
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Blessed is He who has placed in the sky great stars and placed therein a [burning] lamp and luminous moon.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Blessed is the One Who has placed constellations in the sky, as well as a ˹radiant˺ lamp and a luminous moon.

ayahs.textArabic: وَهُوَ ٱلَّذِي جَعَلَ ٱلَّيۡلَ وَٱلنَّهَارَ خِلۡفَةٗ لِّمَنۡ أَرَادَ أَن يَذَّكَّرَ أَوۡ أَرَادَ شُكُورٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wahuwa allathee jaAAala allayla waalnnahara khilfatan liman arada an yaththakkara aw arada shukooran
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And it is He who has made the night and the day in succession for whoever desires to remember or desires gratitude.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And He is the One Who causes the day and the night to alternate, ˹as a sign˺ for whoever desires to be mindful or to be grateful.

ayahs.textArabic: وَعِبَادُ ٱلرَّحۡمَٰنِ ٱلَّذِينَ يَمۡشُونَ عَلَى ٱلۡأَرۡضِ هَوۡنٗا وَإِذَا خَاطَبَهُمُ ٱلۡجَٰهِلُونَ قَالُواْ سَلَٰمٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: WaAAibadu alrrahmani allatheena yamshoona AAala alardi hawnan waitha khatabahumu aljahiloona qaloo salaman
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And the servants of the Most Merciful are those who walk upon the earth easily, and when the ignorant address them [harshly], they say [words of] peace,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: The ˹true˺ servants of the Most Compassionate are those who walk on the earth humbly, and when the foolish address them ˹improperly˺, they only respond with peace.

ayahs.textArabic: وَٱلَّذِينَ يَبِيتُونَ لِرَبِّهِمۡ سُجَّدٗا وَقِيَٰمٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waallatheena yabeetoona lirabbihim sujjadan waqiyaman
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And those who spend [part of] the night to their Lord prostrating and standing [in prayer]
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹They are˺ those who spend ˹a good portion of˺ the night, prostrating themselves and standing before their Lord.

ayahs.textArabic: وَٱلَّذِينَ يَقُولُونَ رَبَّنَا ٱصۡرِفۡ عَنَّا عَذَابَ جَهَنَّمَۖ إِنَّ عَذَابَهَا كَانَ غَرَامًا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waallatheena yaqooloona rabbana isrif AAanna AAathaba jahannama inna AAathabaha kana gharaman
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And those who say, "Our Lord, avert from us the punishment of Hell. Indeed, its punishment is ever adhering;
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹They are˺ those who pray, “Our Lord! Keep the punishment of Hell away from us, for its punishment is indeed unrelenting.

ayahs.textArabic: إِنَّهَا سَآءَتۡ مُسۡتَقَرّٗا وَمُقَامٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Innaha saat mustaqarran wamuqaman
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Indeed, it is evil as a settlement and residence."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: It is certainly an evil place to settle and reside.”

ayahs.textArabic: وَٱلَّذِينَ إِذَآ أَنفَقُواْ لَمۡ يُسۡرِفُواْ وَلَمۡ يَقۡتُرُواْ وَكَانَ بَيۡنَ ذَٰلِكَ قَوَامٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waallatheena itha anfaqoo lam yusrifoo walam yaqturoo wakana bayna thalika qawaman
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And [they are] those who, when they spend, do so not excessively or sparingly but are ever, between that, [justly] moderate
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹They are˺ those who spend neither wastefully nor stingily, but moderately in between.

ayahs.textArabic: وَٱلَّذِينَ لَا يَدۡعُونَ مَعَ ٱللَّهِ إِلَٰهًا ءَاخَرَ وَلَا يَقۡتُلُونَ ٱلنَّفۡسَ ٱلَّتِي حَرَّمَ ٱللَّهُ إِلَّا بِٱلۡحَقِّ وَلَا يَزۡنُونَۚ وَمَن يَفۡعَلۡ ذَٰلِكَ يَلۡقَ أَثَامٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waallatheena la yadAAoona maAAa Allahi ilahan akhara wala yaqtuloona alnnafsa allatee harrama Allahu illa bialhaqqi wala yaznoona waman yafAAal thalika yalqa athaman
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And those who do not invoke with Allah another deity or kill the soul which Allah has forbidden [to be killed], except by right, and do not commit unlawful sexual intercourse. And whoever should do that will meet a penalty.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹They are˺ those who do not invoke any other god besides Allah, nor take a ˹human˺ life—made sacred by Allah—except with ˹legal˺ right, nor commit fornication. And whoever does ˹any of˺ this will face the penalty.

ayahs.textArabic: يُضَٰعَفۡ لَهُ ٱلۡعَذَابُ يَوۡمَ ٱلۡقِيَٰمَةِ وَيَخۡلُدۡ فِيهِۦ مُهَانًا
ayahs.textTransliteration: YudaAAaf lahu alAAathabu yawma alqiyamati wayakhlud feehi muhanan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Multiplied for him is the punishment on the Day of Resurrection, and he will abide therein humiliated -
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Their punishment will be multiplied on the Day of Judgment, and they will remain in it forever, in disgrace.

ayahs.textArabic: إِلَّا مَن تَابَ وَءَامَنَ وَعَمِلَ عَمَلٗا صَٰلِحٗا فَأُوْلَٰٓئِكَ يُبَدِّلُ ٱللَّهُ سَيِّـَٔاتِهِمۡ حَسَنَٰتٖۗ وَكَانَ ٱللَّهُ غَفُورٗا رَّحِيمٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Illa man taba waamana waAAamila AAamalan salihan faolaika yubaddilu Allahu sayyiatihim hasanatin wakana Allahu ghafooran raheeman
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Except for those who repent, believe and do righteous work. For them Allah will replace their evil deeds with good. And ever is Allah Forgiving and Merciful.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: As for those who repent, believe, and do good deeds, they are the ones whose evil deeds Allah will change into good deeds. For Allah is All-Forgiving, Most Merciful.

ayahs.textArabic: وَمَن تَابَ وَعَمِلَ صَٰلِحٗا فَإِنَّهُۥ يَتُوبُ إِلَى ٱللَّهِ مَتَابٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waman taba waAAamila salihan fainnahu yatoobu ila Allahi mataban
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And he who repents and does righteousness does indeed turn to Allah with [accepted] repentance.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And whoever repents and does good has truly turned to Allah properly.

ayahs.textArabic: وَٱلَّذِينَ لَا يَشۡهَدُونَ ٱلزُّورَ وَإِذَا مَرُّواْ بِٱللَّغۡوِ مَرُّواْ كِرَامٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waallatheena la yashhadoona alzzoora waitha marroo biallaghwi marroo kiraman
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And [they are] those who do not testify to falsehood, and when they pass near ill speech, they pass by with dignity.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹They are˺ those who do not bear false witness, and when they come across falsehood, they pass ˹it˺ by with dignity.

ayahs.textArabic: وَٱلَّذِينَ إِذَا ذُكِّرُواْ بِـَٔايَٰتِ رَبِّهِمۡ لَمۡ يَخِرُّواْ عَلَيۡهَا صُمّٗا وَعُمۡيَانٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waallatheena itha thukkiroo biayati rabbihim lam yakhirroo AAalayha summan waAAumyanan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And those who, when reminded of the verses of their Lord, do not fall upon them deaf and blind.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹They are˺ those who, when reminded of the revelation of their Lord, do not turn a blind eye or a deaf ear to it.

ayahs.textArabic: وَٱلَّذِينَ يَقُولُونَ رَبَّنَا هَبۡ لَنَا مِنۡ أَزۡوَٰجِنَا وَذُرِّيَّٰتِنَا قُرَّةَ أَعۡيُنٖ وَٱجۡعَلۡنَا لِلۡمُتَّقِينَ إِمَامًا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waallatheena yaqooloona rabbana hab lana min azwajina wathurriyyatina qurrata aAAyunin waijAAalna lilmuttaqeena imaman
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And those who say, "Our Lord, grant us from among our wives and offspring comfort to our eyes and make us an example for the righteous."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹They are˺ those who pray, “Our Lord! Bless us with ˹pious˺ spouses and offspring who will be the joy of our hearts, and make us models for the righteous.”

ayahs.textArabic: أُوْلَٰٓئِكَ يُجۡزَوۡنَ ٱلۡغُرۡفَةَ بِمَا صَبَرُواْ وَيُلَقَّوۡنَ فِيهَا تَحِيَّةٗ وَسَلَٰمًا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Olaika yujzawna alghurfata bima sabaroo wayulaqqawna feeha tahiyyatan wasalaman
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Those will be awarded the Chamber for what they patiently endured, and they will be received therein with greetings and [words of] peace.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: It is they who will be rewarded with ˹elevated˺ mansions ˹in Paradise˺ for their perseverance, and will be received with salutations and ˹greetings of˺ peace,

ayahs.textArabic: خَٰلِدِينَ فِيهَاۚ حَسُنَتۡ مُسۡتَقَرّٗا وَمُقَامٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Khalideena feeha hasunat mustaqarran wamuqaman
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Abiding eternally therein. Good is the settlement and residence.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: staying there forever. What an excellent place to settle and reside!

ayahs.textArabic: قُلۡ مَا يَعۡبَؤُاْ بِكُمۡ رَبِّي لَوۡلَا دُعَآؤُكُمۡۖ فَقَدۡ كَذَّبۡتُمۡ فَسَوۡفَ يَكُونُ لِزَامَۢا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Qul ma yaAAbao bikum rabbee lawla duAAaokum faqad kaththabtum fasawfa yakoonu lizaman
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Say, "What would my Lord care for you if not for your supplication?" For you [disbelievers] have denied, so your denial is going to be adherent.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Say, ˹O Prophet,˺ “You ˹all˺ would not ˹even˺ matter to my Lord were it not for your faith ˹in Him˺. But now you ˹disbelievers˺ have denied ˹the truth˺, so the torment is bound to come.”
